name: overline_and_double_three_coexist
kind: classification convention difference (not an oracle bug)
facts: Verified independently: (a) the candidate makes a horizontal seven-in-a-row (overline); (b) with the horizontal stones removed, the vertical+diagonal pair is a genuine double-three (detector/Rapfi/renju_forbid all agree). So BOTH forbidden conditions hold at this point.
note: All three tools agree the point is forbidden; there is no disagreement on legality. RIF 9.2 lists overline, double-four and double-three as parallel forbidden reasons, so both reasons are valid here. The single-type outputs differ only by reporting convention: the detector reports a primary reason with priority exact_five > overline > double_four > double_three (hence overline); renju_forbid reports double_three. Neither is wrong on the forbidden/legal question. Recorded as a convention difference; the detector's primary-reason order is the chosen convention.
